design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Network exposure aliasing by finite graph-pair certificate: compare the six-cycle C6 with two disjoint triangles under iid Bernoulli treatment. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Aronow-Samii exposure mappings, Sävje-Aronow-Hudgens interference estimands, and graph-exposure misspecification diagnostics; closest banked proposals are pid_network_exposure_v1 and pid_network_iv_exposure_v1/v2/v3. Our theorem is not those because it gives a concrete non-equivalence certificate: both graphs have identical one-hop treated-neighbor count law Binomial(2,p) at every unit for every p, so any estimator using only the marginal one-hop exposure histogram cannot distinguish them, but their two-hop/triangle-closure exposure covariance differs and induces different spillover estimands under a worked local outcome law. Why non-trivial: the object is not LP bounds or support coverage but a graph-pair aliasing certificate with C6 versus 2K3, explicit Bernoulli exposure tables, and a separating quadratic spillover contrast. Why promising: it creates a finite witness class for exposure-map insufficiency that is invisible to degree/exposure marginals but visible to local dependence geometry. Reject if it becomes routine network-exposure misspecification, partial-ID LP, Manski bounds, or a definition-unfold statement without the graph-pair table and separating contrast.

proposed_from.topic: Network exposure aliasing by finite graph-pair certificate: compare the six-cycle C6 with two disjoint triangles under iid Bernoulli treatment. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Aronow-Samii exposure mappings, Sävje-Aronow-Hudgens interference estimands, and graph-exposure misspecification diagnostics; closest banked proposals are pid_network_exposure_v1 and pid_network_iv_exposure_v1/v2/v3. Our theorem is not those because it gives a concrete non-equivalence certificate: both graphs have identical one-hop treated-neighbor count law Binomial(2,p) at every unit for every p, so any estimator using only the marginal one-hop exposure histogram cannot distinguish them, but their two-hop/triangle-closure exposure covariance differs and induces different spillover estimands under a worked local outcome law. Why non-trivial: the object is not LP bounds or support coverage but a graph-pair aliasing certificate with C6 versus 2K3, explicit Bernoulli exposure tables, and a separating quadratic spillover contrast. Why promising: it creates a finite witness class for exposure-map insufficiency that is invisible to degree/exposure marginals but visible to local dependence geometry. Reject if it becomes routine network-exposure misspecification, partial-ID LP, Manski bounds, or a definition-unfold statement without the graph-pair table and separating contrast.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains pid_network_exposure_v1, whose reviewers requested a concrete finite witness and whose Section 11 left minimal finite strict-containment open. v2 now supplies the C6/2K3 witness and strengthens the open finite issue into a two-regular closure-frontier conjecture rather than a verbal strict-containment claim.","published_axis":"Aronow-Samii and Savje motivate exposure mappings and misspecification but do not give a finite graph-pair alias certificate. Auerbach-Guo-Tabord-Meehan use rooted local configurations broadly, while Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang motivate restricted alternatives through a testing impossibility; v2 targets the epsilon-gap between these threads by naming tau_cl(G) as the finite two-regular radius-two frontier."}
seed_list: Construct a C6-versus-2K3 graph-pair aliasing certificate: identical one-hop Binomial(2,p) count laws but a closure separator changes local quadratic spillover means by eta p^2.
Give a necessary-and-sufficient restricted-alternative diagnostic for C6-versus-2K3 exposure-map testing using triangle closure as the finite certificate.
Characterize the minimal rooted radius-two local configuration needed to distinguish degree-count aliases under iid Bernoulli assignment.
Build a finite two-hop spillover contrast showing bias from one-hop exposure truncation under approximate-neighborhood interference.
Define an exposure-covariance separator for all six-node two-regular graph pairs and compute the induced spillover contrast.
Compare scalar count exposure and rooted configuration exposure as two estimands on the same graph pair and prove the policy-usefulness gap.
Give a graph-pair robustness checklist for multi-network estimators when induced one-hop exposure probabilities agree but closure geometry differs.
Construct a finite witness table for prior pid_network_exposure_v1 strict-containment claims using complete assignment and outcome rows.
Formulate a restricted randomization test whose power is exactly the closure separator on the C6-versus-2K3 class.
Classify when a local quadratic network response surface collapses to a scalar count exposure under degree-regular graphs.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a C6-versus-2K3 graph-pair aliasing certificate: identical one-hop Binomial(2,p) count laws but a closure separator changes local quadratic spillover means by eta p^2.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer demanded a minimal finite witness rather than a verbal containment claim.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Include a minimal four-unit or three-edge witness candidate for Conjecture 1

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/witness pattern: stronger because both graphs are nondegenerate 2-regular laws and the separator is computed.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Aronow-Samii exposure mappings because a richer exposure map can always distinguish graphs.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is not existence of a richer map; it is the finite alias certificate preserving identical one-hop marginal laws while changing a named estimand.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Aronow-Samii 2017 because it gives an evaluated graph-pair non-equivalence certificate, not a general exposure-map framework.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the focal object is a computed closure separator Delta_cl, not support coverage or LP duality.

adversarial_referee.instance: The certificate may be a toy witness without a named focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Delta_cl is exhibited numerically and symbolically in Section 9.

adversarial_referee.instance: A six-node graph pair may be hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: both graphs are nondegenerate 2-regular Bernoulli experiments with all treatment arms active.

adversarial_referee.instance: Savje or Auerbach may already imply this separation.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: they motivate richer exposure structure but do not give the C6/2K3 finite table or eta p^2 contrast.

seed_details.one_line: Give a necessary-and-sufficient restricted-alternative diagnostic for C6-versus-2K3 exposure-map testing using triangle closure as the finite certificate.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer requested the exact certificate family for exposure-map testing.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Add zero-exposure incompatibility constraints to Conjecture 2's certificate formulation

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Testing-impossibility pattern: stronger only if restricted class is finite and certificate is explicit.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is subsumed by Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang because unrestricted tests are impossible.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed restricts the alternative to a finite graph-pair class and asks for a computable certificate.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang 2026 because it narrows the alternative to a named finite graph-pair class.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the diagnostic is the closure certificate; downgrade if it becomes a generic randomization-test restatement.

adversarial_referee.instance: May prove only that closure is sufficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposed theorem includes the radius-one impossibility side.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could misstate unrestricted impossibility as applying to restricted pairs.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft explicitly uses it as motivation for restriction.

adversarial_referee.instance: No estimator or test statistic may be specified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands unless a statistic is added; scored below chosen seed.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the minimal rooted radius-two local configuration needed to distinguish degree-count aliases under iid Bernoulli assignment.

anchor_paper.author: Eric Auerbach, Hongchang Guo and Max Tabord-Meehan
anchor_paper.claim: Rooted local configurations replace scalar count exposures.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/extends exposure summaries: weaker unless minimality is proved for the finite alias class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the local-configuration approach specialized to two small graphs.
seed_details.rebuttal: The finite minimality boundary is not stated in the local-configuration paper.

seed_details.one_line: Build a finite two-hop spillover contrast showing bias from one-hop exposure truncation under approximate-neighborhood interference.

anchor_paper.author: Michael P. Leung
anchor_paper.claim: Approximate neighborhood interference allows smaller but nonzero effects beyond one hop.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends one-hop to two-hop: stronger only through the explicit C6/2K3 closed-form contrast.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine finite example of approximate neighborhood interference.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial part is exact equality of one-hop marginal laws across graphs.

seed_details.one_line: Define an exposure-covariance separator for all six-node two-regular graph pairs and compute the induced spillover contrast.

anchor_paper.author: Bar Weinstein and Daniel Nevo
anchor_paper.claim: Equivalent induced exposure probabilities need not encode all graph geometry.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift: stronger if all two-regular six-node pairs are classified.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is graph enumeration with no econometric content.
seed_details.rebuttal: The separator enters a graph-indexed spillover estimand, not graph isomorphism alone.

seed_details.one_line: Compare scalar count exposure and rooted configuration exposure as two estimands on the same graph pair and prove the policy-usefulness gap.

anchor_paper.author: Eric Auerbach, Jonathan Auerbach and Max Tabord-Meehan
anchor_paper.claim: Misspecified exposure effects are not automatically useful for policy.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Phenomenon-anchor: stronger if policy-usefulness is represented by a computed estimand difference.
seed_details.referee_objection: Policy usefulness is a discussion point, not a theorem target.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed translates the discussion into an exact graph-pair target difference.

seed_details.one_line: Give a graph-pair robustness checklist for multi-network estimators when induced one-hop exposure probabilities agree but closure geometry differs.

anchor_paper.author: Bar Weinstein and Daniel Nevo
anchor_paper.claim: Network misspecification can be studied through induced exposure probabilities and multi-network robustness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator robustness extension: weaker unless the checklist has a necessary failure condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a diagnostic add-on to existing robustness estimators.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is useful only if tied to exact equality of induced one-hop probabilities.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a finite witness table for prior pid_network_exposure_v1 strict-containment claims using complete assignment and outcome rows.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith proposal
anchor_paper.claim: The previous proposal left the minimal strict-containment witness open.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: The exact minimal number of units needed for strict containment is intentionally left open.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/witness: stronger if the table is complete and nondegenerate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This only patches a prior in-repo proposal and lacks published novelty.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; useful as support, not as the main flagship angle.

seed_details.one_line: Formulate a restricted randomization test whose power is exactly the closure separator on the C6-versus-2K3 class.

anchor_paper.author: Tadao Hoshino and Takahide Yanagi
anchor_paper.claim: Randomization tests can target hierarchical spillover specifications.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator/test extension: weaker unless exact finite power is computed.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Hoshino-Yanagi on a small alternative.
seed_details.rebuttal: The exact power object would need to exploit the closure alias separator.

seed_details.one_line: Classify when a local quadratic network response surface collapses to a scalar count exposure under degree-regular graphs.

anchor_paper.author: Fredrik Savje
anchor_paper.claim: Exposure definitions should be separated from structural assumptions about interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption weakening: weaker unless the collapse condition is necessary and sufficient.
seed_details.referee_objection: The collapse condition eta=0 is immediate from the response definition.
seed_details.rebuttal: This is a sanity corollary, not the selected flagship kernel.

proposed_from.literature_map: Hudgens and Halloran (2008, JASA, hudgens_halloran_2008_jasa) define direct and indirect effects under interference. Aronow and Samii (2017, AOAS, aronow_samii_2017_general_interference) make exposure mappings the design-to-estimand bridge. Savje, Aronow and Hudgens (2021, AoS, savje_aronow_hudgens_2021_unknown_interference) study average effects under limited unknown interference. Leung (2022, Econometrica, leung_2022_approximate_neighborhood_interference) allows approximate neighborhood interference beyond exact one-hop truncation. Savje (2024, Biometrika, savje_2024_misspecified_exposure) separates exposure effects from exposure mappings as structural assumptions. Auerbach, Guo and Tabord-Meehan (2026, QE, auerbach_guo_tabordmeehan_2026_local_approach) use rooted local configurations rather than scalar count exposures. Hoshino and Yanagi (2023, arXiv, hoshino_yanagi_2023_specification_test) propose randomization tests for exposure-specification hierarchies. Gao, Harshaw, Savje and Wang (2026, arXiv, gao_harshaw_savje_wang_2026_impossibility_testing) show unrestricted exposure-map testing is impossible without restricted alternatives. Weinstein and Nevo (2026, Biometrics, weinstein_nevo_2026_misspecified_network) study misspecified network interference through induced exposure probabilities. Schroder et al. (2026, arXiv) study sharp bounds for misspecified network exposure mappings but not a finite marginal-histogram alias certificate.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"revise","version":2,"message":"Revised Conjecture 1 from a C6/2K3-only minimality claim into a two-regular closure-frontier conjecture with formal count-equivalence and separation criteria.","proposal_path":"CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair/pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1_proposal.tex","soundness_self_review":{"SC1_hypotheses_in_assumptions":"PASS — §8 cites Assumptions graphs, Bernoulli, consistency, quadratic, and eta; all are present in §7.","SC2_objects_defined":"PASS — §6 now defines S_i^G, C_i^G, mu_G, Delta_cl, tau_cl, count-equivalence, and the separation criterion.","SC3_corollaries_reduce":"PASS — §9 reduces eta=0 to equality because both graphs have E[Z_i]=p and E[S_i^G]=2p.","SC4_intro_kernel_matches_§8":"PASS — §4 P4 names Theorem 1 and Conjecture 1 with statements matching §8.","SC5_consumer_named":"PASS — consumers are Hoshino-Yanagi restricted alternatives, Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang restricted testing, and policy-usefulness diagnostics.","SC6_comparator_promise":"PASS — Aronow-Samii and Savje are delivered by Theorem 1 as finite separator motivation; Auerbach-Guo-Tabord-Meehan and Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang are delivered by Conjecture 1 as a restricted finite frontier.","SC7_no_tautological_iff":"PASS — Conjecture 1 is a minimal additive quotient statement; tau_cl is independently computed from adjacency primitives, not defined as the value at which separation holds.","SC8_tuning_parameter_bounded":"PASS — no free epsilon, gap, or threshold parameter is introduced; the separating scalar is tau_cl(G_T)-tau_cl(G_C)=1 in §9.","SC9_concrete_witness_exists":"PASS — §9 gives the non-corner C6 versus 2K3 witness with p=1/2 and explicit closure values.","SC10_focal_object_exhibited":"Focal object: tau_cl(G) and Delta_cl(p;G_T,G_C), the closure frontier and separator.\\nDefining equation (copy verbatim from Section 6):\\ntau_cl(G) = |V(G)|^{-1} sum_i sum_{j<k} A_ij A_ik A_jk.\\nDelta_cl(p;G_T,G_C) = (1/6) sum_i E{C_i^{G_T}(Z)-C_i^{G_C}(Z)}.\\nRHS symbol provenance:\\n  - p = 1/2     source: Section 6 primitive assignment probability.\\n  - G_C = C6 edges (12,23,34,45,56,61)     source: Section 6 primitive graph.\\n  - G_T = 2K3 edges (12,23,13,45,56,46)     source: Section 6 primitive graph.\\n  - C_i^G(z) = sum_{j<k} A_ij^G A_ik^G A_jk^G z_j z_k     source: Section 6 primitive exposure definition.\\n  - For every i in C6, neighbor-neighbor edge count = 0     source: derived from the Section 6 edge list.\\n  - For every i in 2K3, neighbor-neighbor edge count = 1     source: derived from the Section 6 edge list.\\n  - E[Z_j Z_k] = p^2 = 1/4 for j != k     source: derived from Section 6 iid Bernoulli assignment.\\nSubstituted equation:\\n  tau_cl(G_T)-tau_cl(G_C) = (1/6) * sum_{i=1}^6 (1 - 0)\\n  Delta_cl(1/2;G_T,G_C) = p^2 * {tau_cl(G_T)-tau_cl(G_C)} = (1/4) * 1\\nComputed value:\\n  tau_cl(G_T)-tau_cl(G_C) = 1.\\n  Delta_cl(1/2;G_T,G_C) = 1/4.\\nInvariant check 1: both graphs have six vertices. LHS = |V(C6)| = 6; RHS = |V(2K3)| = 6; result = OK.\\nInvariant check 2: every vertex has degree two. LHS = degree sequence(C6) = (2,2,2,2,2,2); RHS = degree sequence(2K3) = (2,2,2,2,2,2); result = OK.\\nInvariant check 3: one-hop count law agrees. LHS = Binomial(2,1/2); RHS = Binomial(2,1/2); result = OK.\\nInvariant check 4: closure separator denominator is nonzero. LHS = 6; RHS = 6; result = OK.\\nAssumption-substitution screen: No RHS symbol above is sourced from Section 7 assumptions. load-bearing for flagship novelty? NO assumed symbol is load-bearing.\\nSC10 verdict: PASS.","SC11_proof_route_non_trivial":"Headline: tau_cl(G) is the minimal rooted radius-two additive frontier for the two-regular local quadratic count-alias class.\\nStep 0a — unfold Section 6 definitions: expand S_i^G, C_i^G, tau_cl(G), mu_G(p), and Delta_cl; this is not counted in k.\\nStep 0b — apply Section 7 assumptions: use the fixed graph pair, iid Bernoulli assignment, and local quadratic closure response; this is not counted in k.\\nStep 1 (substantive): classify the two-regular count-equivalence class and show the law of S_i^G is always Binomial(2,p), independent of cycle decomposition.\\nStep 2 (substantive): derive the orthogonal decomposition mu_G(p)=alpha+beta p+2 gamma p+eta p^2 tau_cl(G), so all graph dependence in the local quadratic class enters through tau_cl.\\nStep 3 (substantive): prove a minimality lemma for additive rooted radius-two invariants: any invariant preserving all local quadratic means must be constant on the level sets of tau_cl.\\nStep 4 (substantive): show radius-one count summaries cannot recover tau_cl, while the radius-two rooted edge indicator A_ij A_ik A_jk recovers it for every graph in the two-regular class.\\nStep 5 (substantive): verify the non-corner witness C6 versus 2K3 and rule out normalization artifacts by evaluating both tau_cl and Delta_cl.\\nClassical result: finite rooted-neighborhood isomorphism criterion for radius-two graph features closes the sufficiency direction.\\nk = 5; PASS iff k >= 3.\\nSC11 verdict: PASS."},"comparator_promise_table":[{"comparator_bibkey":"aronow_samii_2017_general_interference","comparator_claim":"Exposure mappings link assignment mechanisms to causal estimands under interference.","matched_by":"Theorem 1","match_kind":"strict_tightening"},{"comparator_bibkey":"savje_2024_misspecified_exposure","comparator_claim":"Exposure effects as definitions should be separated from exposure mappings as structural assumptions.","matched_by":"Theorem 1","match_kind":"strict_tightening"},{"comparator_bibkey":"auerbach_guo_tabordmeehan_2026_local_approach","comparator_claim":"Rooted local network configurations organize similarity under network interference.","matched_by":"Conjecture 1","match_kind":"iff_frontier"},{"comparator_bibkey":"gao_harshaw_savje_wang_2026_impossibility_testing","comparator_claim":"Unrestricted exposure-map testing is impossible without restricted alternatives.","matched_by":"Conjecture 1","match_kind":"strict_tightening"}],"novelty_justification":{"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains pid_network_exposure_v1, whose reviewers requested a concrete finite witness and whose Section 11 left minimal finite strict-containment open. v2 now supplies the C6/2K3 witness and strengthens the open finite issue into a two-regular closure-frontier conjecture rather than a verbal strict-containment claim.","published_axis":"Aronow-Samii and Savje motivate exposure mappings and misspecification but do not give a finite graph-pair alias certificate. Auerbach-Guo-Tabord-Meehan use rooted local configurations broadly, while Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang motivate restricted alternatives through a testing impossibility; v2 targets the epsilon-gap between these threads by naming tau_cl(G) as the finite two-regular radius-two frontier."},"literature_checklist":[{"author":"Peter M. Aronow and Cyrus Samii","year":2017,"venue":"Annals of Applied Statistics","bibkey":"aronow_samii_2017_general_interference","claim":"Exposure mappings are the design-to-estimand bridge under interference.","relevant_to":"Theorem 1"},{"author":"Fredrik Savje","year":2024,"venue":"Biometrika","bibkey":"savje_2024_misspecified_exposure","claim":"Exposure effects as definitions should be separated from structural exposure assumptions.","relevant_to":"Theorem 1"},{"author":"Eric Auerbach, Hongchang Guo and Max Tabord-Meehan","year":2026,"venue":"Quantitative Economics / arXiv","bibkey":"auerbach_guo_tabordmeehan_2026_local_approach","claim":"Rooted local configurations pool agents beyond scalar count exposures.","relevant_to":"Conjecture 1"},{"author":"Chao Gao, Christopher Harshaw, Fredrik Savje and Yitan Wang","year":2026,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"gao_harshaw_savje_wang_2026_impossibility_testing","claim":"Unrestricted exposure-mapping tests have worst-case Type I plus Type II error equal to one.","relevant_to":"Conjecture 1"},{"author":"Tadao Hoshino and Takahide Yanagi","year":2023,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"hoshino_yanagi_2023_specification_test","claim":"Randomization tests can compare hierarchical spillover specifications.","relevant_to":"Theorem 1"},{"author":"Michael P. banked_reason: Stopped early after two D-0.5 novelty reviews on the same C6/2K3 closure-separator object: v1 was field-tier/revise and v2 was field-tier/reject because the finite separator was elementary, the comparator chain stayed thin, and the proposed closure frontier did not rise above routine rooted-configuration/network-exposure specialization.
